volumeTitle: The Woman of Andros. The Self-Tormentor. The Eunuch
label: The Self-Tormentor (Section 5.1.947c - Section 5.3.1013)

sections.label: Section 5.1.947c
sections.translation: I do give you leave : is this your desire ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.948b
sections.translation: It is so .

sections.label: Section 5.1.948c
sections.translation: Then be it so .

sections.label: Section 5.1.948d
sections.original: Age iam uxorem ut arcessat paret .
sections.translation: And now let your son prepare to fetch the bride . The other one shall be schooled in such language as befits children . But Syrus —

sections.label: Section 5.1.950b
sections.translation: What of him ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.950c
sections.original: Egone ? si vivo , adeo exornatum dabo ,
sections.translation: What ? If I live , I will have him so handsomely dressed , so well combed out , that he shall always remember me as long as he lives ; to imagine that I ’ m to be a laughing-stock and a plaything for him ! So may the Gods bless me ! he would not have dared to do to a widow-woman the things which he has done to me . [*]

sections.label: Section 5.2.954b
sections.original: Itane tandem quaeso , Menedeme , ut pater
sections.translation: Prithee , is it really the fact , Menedemus , that my father can ,

sections.label: Section 5.2.955
sections.original: Tam in brevi spatio omnem de me eiecerit animum patris ?
sections.translation: in so short a space of time , have cast off all the natural affection of a parent for me ? For what crime ? What so great enormity have I , to my misfortune , committed ? Young men generally do the same .

sections.label: Section 5.2.957b
sections.original: Scio tibi esse hoc gravius multo ac durius ,
sections.translation: I am aware that this must be much more harsh and severe to you , on whom it falls ; but yet I take it no less amiss than you . How it is so I know not , nor can I account for it , except that from my heart I wish you well .

sections.label: Section 5.2.960
sections.translation: Did not you say that my father was waiting here ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.960b
sections.translation: See , here he is .

sections.label: Section 5.2.960c
sections.translation: Why are you blaming me , Clitipho ? Whatever I have done in this matter , I had a view to you and your imprudence . When I saw that you were of a careless disposition , and held the pleasures of the moment of the first importance , and did not look forward to the future , I took measures that you might neither want nor be able to waste this which I have .

sections.label: Section 5.2.965
sections.original: Ubi cui decuit primo tibi non licuit per te mihi dare ,
sections.translation: When , through your own conduct , it was not allowed me to give it you , to whom I ought before all , I had recourse to those who were your nearest relations ; to them I have made over and intrusted every thing . [*] There you ’ ll always find a refuge for your folly ; food , clothing , and a roof under which to betake yourself .

sections.label: Section 5.2.968b
sections.translation: Ah me !

sections.label: Section 5.2.969
sections.original: Satius est quam te ipso herede haec possidere Bacchidem .
sections.translation: It is better than that , you being my heir , Bacchis should possess this estate of mine .

sections.label: Section 5.2.970
sections.original: Disperii : scelestus quantas turbas concivi insciens !
sections.translation: I ’ m ruined irrevocably !— Of what mischief have I , wretch that I am , unthinkingly been the cause ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.971
sections.translation: Would I were dead !

sections.label: Section 5.2.971b
sections.original: Prius quaeso disce quid sit vivere :
sections.translation: Prithee , first learn what it is to live . When you know that , if life displeases you , then try the other .

sections.label: Section 5.2.973
sections.translation: Master , may I be allowed —?

sections.label: Section 5.2.973b
sections.translation: Say on .

sections.label: Section 5.2.973c
sections.translation: But may I safely ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.973d
sections.translation: Say on .

sections.label: Section 5.2.973e
sections.translation: What injustice or what madness is this , that that in which I have offended , should be to his detriment ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.974b
sections.translation: It ’ s all over . [*]

sections.label: Section 5.2.975
sections.original: Ne te admisce : nemo accusat , Syre , te ; nec tu aram tibi
sections.translation: Don ’ t you mix yourself up in it ; no one accuses you , Syrus , nor need you look out for an altar , [*] or for an intercessor for yourself .

sections.label: Section 5.2.976b
sections.translation: What is your design ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.976c
sections.translation: I am not at all angry either with you , ( to SYRUS ) , or with you ( to CLITIPHO ) ; nor is it fair that you should be so with me for what I am doing .

sections.label: Section 5.2.978
sections.translation: He ’ s gone . I wish I had asked him —

sections.label: Section 5.2.978b
sections.translation: What , Syrus ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.978c
sections.translation: Where I am to get my subsistence ; he has so utterly cast us adrift . You are to have it , for the present , at your sister ’ s , I find .

sections.label: Section 5.2.980
sections.original: Adeon rem rediisse ut periculum etiam fame mihi sit , Syre ?
sections.translation: Has it then come to this pass , Syrus — that I am to be in danger even of starving ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.981
sections.translation: So we only live , there ’ s hope —

sections.label: Section 5.2.981b
sections.translation: What hope ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.981c
sections.translation: That we shall be hungry enough .

sections.label: Section 5.2.982
sections.original: Irrides in re tanta , neque me quicquam consilio adiuvas ?
sections.translation: Do you jest in a matter so serious , and not give me any assistance with your advice ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.983
sections.original: Immo et ibi nunc sum , et usque id egi dudum , dum loquitur pater ;
sections.translation: On the contrary , I ’ m both now thinking of that , and have been about it all the time your father was speaking just now ; and so far as I can perceive —

sections.label: Section 5.2.984b
sections.translation: What ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.984c
sections.translation: It will not be wanting long .

sections.label: Section 5.2.985
sections.translation: What is it , then ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.985b
sections.original: Sic est , non esse horum te arbitror .
sections.translation: It is this — I think that you are not their son .

sections.label: Section 5.2.985c
sections.translation: How ’ s that , Syrus ? Are you quite in your senses ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.986b
sections.original: Ego dicam quod mihi in mentem : tu diiudica .
sections.translation: I ’ ll tell you what ’ s come into my mind ; be you the judge . While they had you alone , while they had no other source of joy more nearly to affect them , they indulged you , they lavished upon you . Now a daughter has been found , a pretense has been found in fact on which to turn you adrift .

sections.label: Section 5.2.990
sections.translation: It ’ s very probable .

sections.label: Section 5.2.990b
sections.original: An tu ob peccatum hoc esse illum iratum putas ?
sections.translation: Do you suppose that he is so angry on account of this fault ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.991
sections.translation: I do not think so .

sections.label: Section 5.2.991b
sections.original: Nunc aliud specta : matres omnes filiis
sections.translation: Now consider another thing . All mothers are wont to be advocates for their sons when in fault , and to aid them against a father ’ s severity ; ’ tis not so here .

sections.label: Section 5.2.993b
sections.original: Verum dicis : quid nunc faciam , Syre ?
sections.translation: You say true ; what then shall I now do , Syrus ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.994
sections.original: Suspicionem istanc ex illis quaere ; rem profer palam :
sections.translation: Question them on this suspicion ; mention the matter without reserve ;

sections.label: Section 5.2.995
sections.original: Si non est verum , ad misericordiam ambos adduces cito , aut
sections.translation: either , if it is not true , you ’ ll soon bring them both to compassion , or else you ’ ll soon find out whose son you are .

sections.label: Section 5.2.996b
sections.translation: You give good advice ; I ’ ll do so .

sections.label: Section 5.2.996c
sections.translation: Most fortunately did this come into my mind . For the less hope the young man entertains , the greater the difficulty with which he ’ ll bring his father to his own terms . I ’ m not sure even , that he may not take a wife , and then no thanks for Syrus .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1000
sections.original: Quid hoc autem ? senex exit foras : ego fugio : adhuc quod factum est
sections.translation: But what is this ? The old man ’ s coming out of doors ; I ’ ll be off . What has so far happened , I am surprised at , that he didn ’ t order me to becarried off from here : now I ’ ll away to Menedemus here , I ’ ll secure him as my intercessor ; I can put no trust in our old man .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1003
sections.original: Profecto nisi caves tu homo , aliquid gnato conficies mali :
sections.translation: Really , sir , if you don ’ t take care , you ’ ll be causing some mischief to your son ; and indeed I do wonder at it ,

sections.label: Section 5.3.1005
sections.original: Tam ineptum quicquam tibi in mentem venire , mi vir , potuerit .
sections.translation: my husband , how anything so foolish could ever come into your head .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1006
sections.original: Oh , pergin mulier esse ? Nullamne ego rem unquam in vita mea
sections.translation: Oh , you persist in being the woman ? Did I ever wish for any one thing in all my life , Sostrata , but that you were my contradicter on that occasion ? And yet if I were now to ask you what it is that I have done amiss , or why you act thus , you would not know in what point you are now so obstinately opposing me in your folly .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1009b
sections.translation: I , not know ?

sections.label: Section 5.3.1010
sections.original: Immo scis , potius quam quidem redeat ad integrum haec eadem oratio .
sections.translation: Yes , rather , I should have said you do know ; inasmuch as either expression amounts to the same thing . [*]

sections.label: Section 5.3.1011
sections.original: Oh , iniquus es , qui me tacere de re tanta postules .
sections.translation: Alas ! you are unreasonable to expect me to be silent in a matter of such importance .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1012
sections.original: Non postulo : iam loquere : nihilo minus ego hoc faciam tamen .
sections.translation: I don ’ t expect it ; talk on then , I shall still do it not a bit the less .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1013
sections.translation: Will you do it ?
